description_long: This object is a ground-mounted solar panel array, elevated on a single sturdy metal pole anchored into a circular patch of grass. The array consists of twelve rectangular photovoltaic panels arranged in a 3x4 grid, each with visible blue-black solar cells and a metallic frame. The panel assembly is supported by a robust metal structure, including angled braces and a central support beam, providing stability and optimal tilt for sunlight capture. The mounting pole is square in cross-section and appears to be made of galvanized steel, ensuring durability against weather. The underside of the panel reveals a network of support struts and a clean, industrial finish, with no visible cabling or inverter components. The overall design is functional and modern, suitable for installation in parks, open urban spaces, or near public amenities.
description: A ground-mounted solar panel array on a single metal pole, featuring twelve blue-black panels in a 3x4 grid above a grassy base. The structure is robust, with a metallic frame and angled supports for stability.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the 3x4 solar panel grid and the supporting pole centered on a grassy patch.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tilt angle of the panel array and the angled support braces attached to the pole.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the metallic support structure beneath the panels and the back of the pole.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the tilt and the structural supports, with the pole and shadow visible.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, facing forward (likely south), orientation appears fixed but may be adjustable via brackets
attribute_mounting_structure_type: pole-mounted, supported by a steel or aluminum post with angled metal braces
attribute_array_configuration: 3x4 grid of solar panels arranged in a single rectangular array
